authors_text: Piotr Mirowski,Dylan Banarse,Mateusz Malinowski,Simon Osindero,Chrisantha Fernando
title: CLIP-CLOP: CLIP-Guided Collage and Photomontage
title_zh: CLIP-CLOP：CLIP 引导的拼贴和蒙太奇
comment: 5 pages, 7 figures, published at the International Conference on Computational Creativity (ICCC) 2022 as Short Paper: Demo
comments: 5 pages, 7 figures, published at the International Conference on Computational Creativity (ICCC) 2022 as Short Paper: Demo
abstract: The unabated mystique of large-scale neural networks, such as the CLIP dual image-and-text encoder, popularized automatically generated art. Increasingly more sophisticated generators enhanced the artworks' realism and visual appearance, and creative prompt engineering enabled stylistic expression. Guided by an artist-in-the-loop ideal, we design a gradient-based generator to produce collages. It requires the human artist to curate libraries of image patches and to describe (with prompts) the whole image composition, with the option to manually adjust the patches' positions during generation, thereby allowing humans to reclaim some control of the process and achieve greater creative freedom. We explore the aesthetic potentials of high-resolution collages, and provide an open-source Google Colab as an artistic tool.
summary: The unabated mystique of large-scale neural networks, such as the CLIP dual image-and-text encoder, popularized automatically generated art. Increasingly more sophisticated generators enhanced the artworks' realism and visual appearance, and creative prompt engineering enabled stylistic expression. Guided by an artist-in-the-loop ideal, we design a gradient-based generator to produce collages. It requires the human artist to curate libraries of image patches and to describe (with prompts) the whole image composition, with the option to manually adjust the patches' positions during generation, thereby allowing humans to reclaim some control of the process and achieve greater creative freedom. We explore the aesthetic potentials of high-resolution collages, and provide an open-source Google Colab as an artistic tool.
abstract_zh: 大规模神经网络（例如 CLIP 双图像和文本编码器）的神秘性有增无减，普及了自动生成艺术。日益复杂的生成器增强了艺术品的真实感和视觉外观，而创造性的提示工程则实现了风格表达。在艺术家循环理念的指导下，我们设计了一个基于梯度的生成器来生成拼贴画。它要求人类艺术家管理图像补丁库并描述（带有提示）整个图像合成，并可以选择在生成过程中手动调整补丁的位置，从而允许人类重新获得对过程的一些控制并实现更大的创作自由。我们探索高分辨率拼贴画的美学潜力，并提供开源的 Google Colab 作为艺术工具。

AI.tldr: 本文提出CLIP-CLOP方法，利用CLIP模型生成高质量拼贴画和照片蒙太奇，结合艺术家的创意指导与手动调整。
AI.motivation: 尽管大模型如CLIP推动了自动艺术生成，但现有方法缺乏艺术家对创作过程的控制，需通过交互式工具提升创作自由度。
AI.method: 设计基于梯度的生成器，要求艺术家预设图像块库并输入文本提示描述整体构图，支持生成过程中手动调整图像块位置。
AI.result: 探索高分辨率拼贴画的美学可能性，开源Google Colab工具供艺术创作使用。
AI.conclusion: 该方法使艺术家在生成流程中重新获得控制权，验证了CLIP在艺术创作中的潜力，为交互式生成艺术提供新方向。

search_text: CLIP-CLOP: CLIP-Guided Collage and Photomontage CLIP-CLOP：CLIP 引导的拼贴和蒙太奇 The unabated mystique of large-scale neural networks, such as the CLIP dual image-and-text encoder, popularized automatically generated art. Increasingly more sophisticated generators enhanced the artworks' realism and visual appearance, and creative prompt engineering enabled stylistic expression. Guided by an artist-in-the-loop ideal, we design a gradient-based generator to produce collages. It requires the human artist to curate libraries of image patches and to describe (with prompts) the whole image composition, with the option to manually adjust the patches' positions during generation, thereby allowing humans to reclaim some control of the process and achieve greater creative freedom. We explore the aesthetic potentials of high-resolution collages, and provide an open-source Google Colab as an artistic tool. 大规模神经网络（例如 CLIP 双图像和文本编码器）的神秘性有增无减，普及了自动生成艺术。日益复杂的生成器增强了艺术品的真实感和视觉外观，而创造性的提示工程则实现了风格表达。在艺术家循环理念的指导下，我们设计了一个基于梯度的生成器来生成拼贴画。它要求人类艺术家管理图像补丁库并描述（带有提示）整个图像合成，并可以选择在生成过程中手动调整补丁的位置，从而允许人类重新获得对过程的一些控制并实现更大的创作自由。我们探索高分辨率拼贴画的美学潜力，并提供开源的 Google Colab 作为艺术工具。 本文提出CLIP-CLOP方法，利用CLIP模型生成高质量拼贴画和照片蒙太奇，结合艺术家的创意指导与手动调整。 Piotr Mirowski,Dylan Banarse,Mateusz Malinowski,Simon Osindero,Chrisantha Fernando cs.CV,cs.AI
